title: The Civil War
description: Test your knowledge of the events of the Civil War.

assessment_items.question_content:
The eleven states that seceded from the Union in 1860-61 formed a new political entity called the 





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Articles of Confederation were the original constitution of the United States.

choices.content: Confederate States of America.
Iroquois Confederacy.
Articles of Confederation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Abraham Lincoln**, who was elected president in 1860, represented 





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Abraham Lincoln was not a member of the Democratic Party.

choices.content: the anti-slavery Republican Party.
the Constitutional Union Party.
the pro-slavery Democratic Party.

hints: The Emancipation Proclamation didn’t free all slaves right away.

choices.content: freed all enslaved people living in territories then in rebellion against the United States.
freed all enslaved people everywhere in the United States.
freed all enslaved people living in the Border States of Kentucky, Delaware, Maryland, and Missouri.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Thirteenth Amendment**, passed in January 1865, 



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Eighteenth Amendment, not the Thirteenth, prohibited the sale or consumption of alcohol in the United States.

choices.content: Abolished the institution of slavery in the United States.
Granted African American men the right to vote.
Prohibited the sale or consumption of alcohol in the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Battle of Bull Run**, fought in April 1861, was notable because





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Battle of Bull Run was held on American soil; no submarines were involved.

choices.content: It was a clear Confederate victory, signaling to the United States that the Civil War would be considerably longer and more difficult than expected.
It was a clear United States victory, demonstrating that the military and industrial capacity of Northern states would quickly overwhelm the rebellion.
It marked the first use of submarine warfare in the famous battle between the *Monitor* and the *Merrimack.*

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Battle of Gettysburg** in July 1863 was a major turning point in the Civil War because





[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The victory of US forces ended the attempted Confederate invasion of the North.
It marked the first time that African American soldiers fought for the United States.
The defeat of US forces soured public opinion about the war, leading to Lincoln’s defeat in the election of 1864.

assessment_items.question_content:
Why did actor **John Wilkes Booth** assassinate Abraham Lincoln? 





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Nothing in historical records suggests that Booth was mentally ill.

choices.content: Lincoln gave a speech announcing his intention to extend citizenship to African Americans, and Booth was a Southern sympathizer and racist.
Booth was a madman who had lost touch with reality; he believed he was assassinating Julius Caesar as his character had done on the stage.
Booth actually intended to kill Lincoln’s theatre companion Major Henry Rathbone in a duel over Clara Harris; he shot Lincoln by mistake.

hints: The meeting at the Appomattox Courthouse was not actually a battle; one of the armies realized they did not have the manpower to engage in a fight.

choices.content: Robert E. Lee surrendered to Ulysses S. Grant, effectively ending the Civil War.
Confederate forces won a surprising victory that extended the Civil War until November.
Ulysses S. Grant surrendered to Robert E. Lee, leaving William Tecumseh Sherman to win the Civil War with his March to the Sea.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sherman’s March to the Sea in the fall of 1864 was an early example of 



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Although some Confederate leaders considered turning to guerrilla warfare after defeat, Robert E. Lee counseled them to make a clean end to the war.

choices.content: total war, designed to demoralize and deplete the civilian resources of the enemy.
trench warfare, which would become standard in World War I.
guerrilla warfare, which led to pockets of ongoing Confederate resistance well into the 1870s.

assessment_items.question_content:
What major political event leading up to the Civil War took place while **James Buchanan** was a lame duck president?



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Mexican-American War took place before James Buchanan’s presidency (when James K. Polk was president).

choices.content: The seven states of the Deep South seceded from the Union and formed the Confederate States of America.
The passage of the Fugitive Slave Act
The Mexican-American War

assessment_items.question_content:
White Southerners choosing to fire on Union-held **Fort Sumter** is considered to be





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The South managed to capture Fort Sumter, so it was not a Union victory.

choices.content: the official start of the Civil War.
the most atrocious act of the Civil War.
the greatest Union victory of the Civil War.

assessment_items.question_content:
Why did some people frown on Lincoln’s appearance and speech at **Gettysburg** at the time?





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Union secured a major victory at Gettysburg.

choices.content: They thought he was using the dedication of the cemetery for the fallen soldiers as a political opportunity.
They thought he was personally responsible for the Union defeat at Gettysburg.
They thought Lincoln should be focusing on commanding the armies of the United States, not attending cemetery dedications.
